name: Pho Bac (Hanoi Beef & Rice-Noodle Soup)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Hot chili sauce (tuong ot) or nuoc cham
recipeInstructions:
1. Leave onion unpeeled and stud with eight whole cloves.
2. Freeze the pieces of meat for 30 minutes before slicing to ensure paper-thin slices.
3. The night before, clean bones under cold running water and soak overnight in a pot with water at room temperature. This helps loosen impurities that release faster when heat is applied, producing a clear broth.
4. Place beef bones, oxtails, and short rib plate in a large stockpot. Add enough water to cover and bring to a boil. Cook for 10 minutes, then drain. Rinse the pot and bones.
5. Return bones to the pot and add 6 quarts of water. Bring to a boil and skim surface foam and fat. Continue simmering, stirring occasionally to free impurities. Skim until foam stops rising. Add more water (3 quarts) and bring to a boil. Remove residue on top, then turn heat to low.
6. Simmer with charred onions, shallots, ginger, star anise, cinnamon stick, parsnips, and salt for 1 hour.
7. Remove short rib plates, set meat aside, and return bones to the pot. Continue simmering uncovered for 4-5 hours, adding water as needed.
8. Slice beef sirloin against grain into paper-thin pieces (2 by 2 inches). Slice reserved short rib meat similarly. Set aside.
9. Combine scallions, coriander, and half sliced onions in a bowl. Mix remaining sliced onions with hot chili sauce in another bowl.
10. Soak rice sticks in warm water for 30 minutes, then drain.
11. When broth is ready, remove bones and strain through cheesecloth-lined strainer into a clean pot. Add fish sauce and bring to a boil, reducing heat to maintain a simmer.
12. Boil noodles in separate pot, drain, divide among bowls, and top with sliced meats. Return boiling broth directly over meat in each bowl (heat cooks raw beef instantly). Garnish with scallion mixture and pepper. Serve onions in chili sauce and accompaniments on the side for diners to add as desired.
